transition_zone_cool_season_pressure.topic: Transition zone cool season regional pressure calendar
spring_pressures: spring dead spots and root carryover stress
early broadleaf emergence and crabgrass program timing
summer_pressures: pythium blight during hot wet stretches
anthracnose and summer decline on stressed greens
scouting_focus: leaf wetness and nighttime weather shifts
timing_biases: Let weather lead disease prevention timing.
Treat fall as the main Poa and recovery planning window.
Scout moisture inconsistency before visual collapse.

northern_cool_season_pressure.topic: Northern cool season regional pressure calendar
spring_pressures: spring dead tissue and compaction carryover
early crabgrass and broadleaf timing decisions
scouting_focus: late-summer grub and root pruning evidence
timing_biases: Use fall as the strongest renovation and prevention window.
Do not let winter pressure surprise you because summer looked clean.
Use scouting to separate recovery needs from fertility needs.

southeast_warm_season_pressure.topic: Southeast warm season regional pressure calendar
fall_pressures: weed escape into thin or traffic-stressed turf
transition planning where overseeding is used
winter_pressures: traffic stress on dormant or semi-dormant turf
scouting_focus: turns, exits, and shaded damp zones
organic matter buildup and soft corridors
timing_biases: Scout weekly during peak heat because pressure shifts quickly.
Treat thatch and moisture as part of pest and disease pressure management.
Do not wait for visible thinning before checking insects.

arid_west_bermudagrass_pressure.topic: Arid west bermudagrass regional pressure calendar
summer_pressures: localized dry spot and moisture variability
traffic stress on dry shoulders and hot spots
summer annual weed escape where coverage is thin
timing_biases: Treat salinity and moisture as calendar-long tracking categories.
Base transition decisions on bermudagrass recovery status, not just appearance.
Use uniformity audits before assuming chemistry failed.
